rule: Highways and major arterial roads cannot be modified or relocated without federal environmental impact assessment
Parks and public green spaces must maintain a minimum of 30% permeable surface to support groundwater recharge
All construction within designated flood zones must be elevated a minimum of 2 feet above the base flood elevation
Residential zones must maintain a minimum green coverage ratio of 20% per lot; dense housing developments require environmental offset
New public infrastructure projects over $5M must include a renewable energy component covering at least 15% of projected energy use
Construction activities in air quality non-attainment zones require a dust and emissions management plan approved by the environmental authority
Public transit corridors must maintain a minimum 15-metre right-of-way clearance and cannot be obstructed by permanent structures
Any development within 100 metres of a heritage-listed building requires heritage impact assessment and approval from the city heritage board
Stormwater management systems must be designed to handle a 1-in-100-year storm event; retention basins are required for developments over 2 hectares
All new commercial and multi-residential buildings must achieve a minimum Green Star 4-star equivalent rating or demonstrate equivalent sustainability outcomes
